Description: Reminder feature fixed, /version command, /location command, few visual changes
Fee split based on IN list, minor notification error fixes, server timezone fixes, critical roll call end fix
Multiple Roll Call (MRC) support, /individual_fee command
Bug and visual fixes
Major release with Docker support and DB foundation
Major release with SQLite/PostgreSQL support and UI/UX overhaul
Stats enabled for real users only, user order and proxy panel fixes
Templates for quick roll call creation, end confirmation message
Major ordering revamp, stats collection for future insights, minor bug fixes
Dupcheck removed, deleteuser supports username, OpenClaw+Claude prompt support
Logging enhanced, @none tag fix, UI/UX improvements
Bug fixes & security hardening: improved error handling for /version and broadcast commands, fixed proxy owner data loss, safer username and limit handling, errors now properly logged
Resolve set_location UnboundLocalError and add handler tests
Use GH_PAT for version bump push to bypass branch protection
Verify GH_PAT end-to-end version bump

Major release: Ghost Tracking feature. Bot now tracks session no-shows per user. After /erc, organiser is asked if anyone ghosted — tap to select from the IN list. Repeat ghosts get a reconfirmation prompt when voting IN again. New commands: /absent_stats (public leaderboard), /set_absent_limit, /mark_absent, /clear_absent, /toggle_ghost_tracking. Ghost tracking is enabled by default with a limit of 1 missed session.
Ghost Tracking — session no-show tracking with reconfirmation (v5.0)
Ghost Tracking — fix proxy user (/sif) ghost count tracking and support /stats ghost, /clear_absent commands for proxy users

Ghost feature fixes: absent_limit threshold now correctly loaded from DB on restart (cache key mismatch), ghost prompt now fires on panel end (btn_endconfirm) not just /erc command, ghost selections restored from DB on bot restart preventing silent data loss

Enhancements: webhook mode support (WEBHOOK_URL env var), attendance rate + streak display in /stats, /history command (last 10 ended sessions), /delete_user confirmation keyboard, attendance streak tracking (current & best), rate limiting on /in /out /maybe, ghost prompt after auto-close, streak updates on event end
/buzz command: admin can ping group members who haven't voted yet; if no rollcall is running, pings all known members. Supports ::N for multi-rollcall targeting and optional custom message.

In-place panel editing (#9): /in, /out, /maybe commands now edit the existing rollcall panel instead of posting a new message. Falls back to a new panel send if the original was deleted or expired. Panel message_id tracked per rollcall; cleaned up on rollcall end.

/buzz rework: member list persisted in new chat_members DB table (no in-memory state). Members recorded on every vote with fresh display names. Before each buzz, concurrently verifies group membership via Telegram API and marks leavers inactive so they are never pinged again. No duplicate pings — table primary key guarantees one record per user per chat.

Scheduled templates: /schedule_template command lets admins enable recurring weekly auto-start for individual templates. Rollcall opens at configured schedule_day+time and closes at the template's event_day+time. Persistent scheduler loop runs at bot startup, fires per-chat in the group's timezone, deduplicates via last_scheduled_date. Ghost tracking fix: _migrate_schema no longer stamps active rollcalls as absent_marked on every restart. toggle_ghost_tracking now accepts explicit true/false/on/off args. Migration adds ghost_tracking_enabled and schedule columns to existing DBs.

Code review hardening: background tasks now surface exceptions via _log_task_exc done-callback; /buzz membership check uses asyncio.wait_for(timeout=5s) so slow API responses don't block; legacy database.json write removed from start_roll_call; /sif rejects duplicate proxy before any DB write; /set_template partial-update preserves existing fields; /set_template first-token fix prevents key=value args being parsed as titles; /erc and panel-end renumber message now shows specific old→new ID mapping instead of generic text.

Bug fixes H1/H2/H3: per-chat asyncio.Lock in manager prevents concurrent /erc double-end; deleting a proxy user now also removes their ghost_records row so they no longer appear on /absent_stats; proxy ghost events now record user_name in ghost_events table for complete audit trail.

Bug fixes M1/L1-L4: bare except replaced with logged Exception throughout; roll_call_not_started errors now surface in logs; user IN-position reset on OUT/MAYBE vote so re-joining IN gets correct order; status validation added to add_or_update_user/proxy; template auto-start failures logged per-template without blocking other templates.

6 new features: (1) Waitlist auto-promotion DM — real users and proxy owners get a private DM when promoted from WAITING→IN; (2) Admin audit log — new admin_actions table records every admin action; /audit_log command to view history; (3) /buzz rate limiting — 30s per-chat cooldown with remaining-time feedback; (4) /history pagination — /history [count] [page] with see-more hint; (5) Custom template recurrence — biweekly and monthly auto-start options in /schedule_template; (6) Manual status override — /set_status <name> <in|out|maybe> [::N] with inline confirmation.

Silent Mode & Stability Hardening: (1) Truly Silent Mode — /shh now suppresses all confirmation 'ack' messages and prioritizes silent in-place panel edits to eliminate pings; (2) Silenced Settings — title, limit, location, and fee updates are now silent in shh mode; (3) Reminder Loop Fix — resolved bug where auto-started templates failed to auto-close/remind; (4) ID Snapshot Fix — corrected shifting indices in auto-close messages to ensure accurate RC IDs; (5) Silence Noise Reduction — suppressed noisy ID re-mapping and auto-start pings in Silent Mode.

🔧 Bot Freeze Fix — bot no longer freezes at night or mid-day; silently-dead TCP connections now cleared every 5 min, poll timeout reduced to 35 s for faster recovery, expired button-tap errors quietly suppressed. 📋 Audit Log 2.0 — /audit_log rebuilt with paginated ← page → navigation (15/page default), compact one-liner entries showing rollcall name instead of shifting ID, 6 new tracked actions (new rollcall, end rollcall, buzz, quiet mode on/off, timezone change, panel end), panel end-rollcall was completely unlogged before. 🕐 Auto-Close Fix — scheduled template rollcalls now correctly close at event time even after a bot restart; reminder loops resume on startup so nothing is silently skipped. 🐛 Edge-case fix: get_next_weekday_datetime returned next-week date when schedule and event time were identical; double minute-alignment delay in reminder loop removed.

🔊 Louder Mode Fixes — /sif, /sof, /smf proxy commands now send correct ack messages in louder mode (dead code path fixed: addIn() returns None on success, not a User object); /in and /maybe also show 'X is now IN/MAYBE!' acks; all proxy command messages properly gated by shh mode. 📌 Panel Debounce — in louder mode, panel sends are debounced 5 minutes so rapid votes produce one panel update instead of flooding the chat; implemented with non-blocking asyncio.sleep. 💾 Panel Persistence — panel_msg_id column added to rollcalls table and lazily restored on restart so in-place edits continue working after a bot reboot. 🐛 SQLite cursor leak fix in get_all_chat_ids().

🚀 Auto Panel on Start — /src and scheduled template auto-starts now immediately send the full inline vote panel (instead of plain text), so members can vote without any extra steps. 🎉 Ended-by Attribution — /erc and panel end now append 'Ended by <name>' to the finish message. 🐛 Production fix: panel_msg_id was never actually persisted (name 'db' is not defined error in _persist_panel_msg_id — update_rollcall was not imported as a function); panel persistence now works correctly across restarts. 🛡️ Guard: _dm_promoted_real_user is now only called when user_id is an int (all 5 call sites guarded), preventing silent errors for proxy users promoted from waitlist. 📌 Debounce cancel on end — pending panel debounce tasks are now cancelled when a rollcall ends (/erc or panel end) to prevent stale panel sends. ⚡ Rate limit on panel buttons — btn_in/out/maybe callbacks now apply the same rate-limit check as the equivalent commands.

🏗️ Major code refactoring for effective LLM token utilization — telegram_helper.py split into focused handler modules (core, voting, lifecycle, settings, ghost, admin, lists, proxy, templates, stats); each module is single-responsibility and significantly smaller, reducing token overhead for AI-assisted development and code review. 🐛 7 bug fixes: 3-segment timezone parsing (/timezone America/Indiana/Indianapolis), string-vs-int comparison in /reminder, uncaught exceptions in /wait_limit and /set_rollcall_time, ghost reconf double-counting stats on comment update, SQL aggregate column alias mismatch (SQLite vs PostgreSQL), traceback print statements replaced with structured logging throughout.

🛡️ Markdown injection hardening — usernames/titles with _, *, `, [ no longer crash Telegram parse_mode='Markdown' sends. New _esc_md() and format_mention_with_name_md() helpers in bot_state applied across stats, lifecycle, voting, proxy, admin, ghost, settings, templates, lists, check_reminders. Fixes production 'can't parse entities' errors for users like @Nasri_Gunner. 📖 /help split — /help shows user commands, /help admin shows admin commands; cleaner discovery and shorter scroll. 🕐 IST log timestamps — TZ=Asia/Kolkata in docker-compose so log timestamps and datetime.now() match the operator's timezone. 🐛 4 bug fixes from deep audit: (1) stale wait_pos/out_pos after promotion caused unfair waitlist ordering — positions now reset when leaving a bucket so re-entry is FIFO at the back; (2) auto-close vs /erc race condition — check_reminders now takes the same erc_lock and re-verifies before double-sending finish text or ghost prompt; (3) /maybe and /smf were missing notify_proxy_owner_wait_to_in for WAITLIST→IN promotions — now consistent with /out, /sof, panel, /set_limit; (4) stats._esc() None guard; bare except in models.delete_user replaced with except Exception. ⚙️ Misc: 429 retry-on-error, panel persistence DB migrations, debounce removed (immediate panel updates).

🛡️ Hardening release — 17 bug fixes + reliability pass. ✅ /stats attendance rate now works (was always blank). 💬 /out shows a confirmation message like /in and /maybe. 🤫 Panel-end respects /shh mode. 🔠 Display names with ] no longer break formatting. 🧹 /delete_user refuses to wipe two users sharing a first name — use @username to disambiguate. 📨 Users moved IN→WAITLIST by /set_limit now get a DM. 🔁 /set_status is a no-op if you're already in that bucket (no more list reorder). 📋 Cleaner command menu — regular members see user commands, admins see admin commands. 🔒 Votes that race with /erc fail cleanly instead of silently breaking. 🩺 /health reports background-task liveness and DB pool usage. 🐛 Plus many internal fixes for memory growth, panel recovery after restart, and proxy-comment loss in ghost prompts.

👻 Ghost feature polish — (1) confirmed attendance now decrements ghost_count by 1 (floored at 0) when admin finalises /mark_absent, so users who show up earn their way out of the reconfirmation threshold without needing /clear_absent; (2) reconfirmation prompt no longer duplicates — spamming /in (or the panel IN button) while a prompt is already pending is silently ignored, and the prompt is skipped entirely if the user is already on the IN list; (3) the warning message now @-mentions the user by name on both the /in path and the panel-button path so it's clear who it's directed at. 🧹 Memory hygiene — abandoned reconfirmation prompts (user ignored the buttons) now carry a _ts and are pruned by the memory_prune_loop after 1h, so the dict no longer leaks until restart. 📣 /buzz upgrades — (a) new tracking middleware records every real user on any group message or button tap, not just on /in/out/maybe votes, so /buzz finally reaches lurkers who have never voted; (b) defensive dedup of the mention list guarantees one ping per user per buzz even if the chat_members table ever ends up with duplicate rows; (c) when multiple rollcalls are active, /buzz (no ::N) now spares anyone who voted on ANY of them — admins can still target one with /buzz ::N. Message text adapts: per-rollcall title when targeted or only one rollcall is open, otherwise lists all active rollcall titles. 🛠️ Duplicate-vote message fix — /in, /out, /maybe used to reply with 'No duplicate proxy please :-)' for real-user re-votes, which made no sense to non-admin members. New alreadyInList exception now gives 'You're already IN / OUT / MAYBE for <title>.' with the actual bucket and rollcall name. /sif, /sof, /smf still use duplicateProxy since those really are proxy-name collisions.
